evidence.reason: COMP-0025 and the architecture/development documents identify graph-local scoped memory as a missing capability. Current implementation validates bootstrap edge records, formats artifact lineage/source trees, and exposes future edge seeds, but context construction still relies on latest_records_by_context_level and does not compute graph neighborhoods or graph-state-aware memory selection.

name: Graph-local scoped memory context selection
notes: The original recommended first slice, adding a read-only graph neighborhood diagnostic/advisor, was completed by PLAN-20260709-0002 and recorded as CAP-0005. GAP-0002 remains open because graph-local scoped memory context selection is not yet implemented.
recommended_first_slice: Use CAP-0006 evidence-only reports to decide whether a separate context-pack integration proposal should be created for graph-local scoped memory. Any integration must be a new governed plan and must explicitly preserve existing default context-pack behavior until admitted. Do not modify context selection, provider prompts, persisted graph artifacts, retrieval, embeddings, reranking, production indexes, memory stores, runtime manifests, or automatic context inclusion without a separate admission warrant.
risk: Without graph-local scoped memory, context assembly may remain too coarse: it can miss relevant neighboring artifacts, include stale latest artifacts, or fail to distinguish local task context from global ontology/invariant context.
summary: AI-Lab cannot yet construct context from graph-local neighborhoods, typed relation records, scoped summaries, and local/global memory balance. Current latest-context selection remains artifact-type based, with edge validation and edge-seed support but no graph-state or neighborhood selection mechanism.
